name: Extremaduran Spanish (Extremeño)

ancestors.notes: Western Andalusian influence: aspiration of /s/, some seseo zones in southeastern Badajoz

notes: Extremaduran Spanish (Extremeño). Spoken in Extremadura (Cáceres and Badajoz provinces). Transitional between Castilian and Andalusian. Strong Leonese substrate influence in northern Cáceres (Jerte Valley, Sierra de Gata). KEY FEATURES: (1) S-ASPIRATION: word-final /s/ → [h] or deleted, especially in Badajoz (Andalusian influence): más [mah], estos [ehtoh]. More preserved in Cáceres. (2) YEÍSMO: /ʎ/ merged with /j/ (as in standard Castilian and most Spanish varieties). (3) SESEO ZONES: southeastern Badajoz (Zafra, Jerez de los Caballeros) shows seseo: cien=sien, zapato=[saˈpato]; rest of Extremadura maintains distinción. (4) X-WEAKENING: /x/ may aspirate to [h] before back vowels, especially in relaxed speech. (5) INTERVOCALIC D-DELETION: final -d and intervocalic -d- frequently deleted: comida → [koˈmiə], ciudad → [θjuˈða] or [θjuˈa]. (6) LEONESE SUBSTRATE (northern Cáceres): traces of diphthongization and consonantal features of Asturleonese; the Jerte/Ambroz valleys historically Leonese-speaking. (7) PORTUGUESE CONTACT: Badajoz border areas show Portuguese contact features (stronger open vowels, some nasal vowel awareness). (8) NOT EXTREMADURAN LANGUAGE: Distinct from the Extremaduran language (ext), an Asturleonese variety; this file models the Spanish spoken in Extremadura.

sources.author: Penny, R.
sources.title: A History of the Spanish Language
